subject: Ecology
title: Invasive Predators on Islands
description: A micro-lesson that makes Invasive Predators on Islands usable.

hook.text: Rats, cats, and stoats have caused more island extinctions than any other threat.

buildup.text: Island species evolved without mammalian predators and have no defenses against them.

discovery.text: A single pair of cats released on an island can devastate bird populations within years.

twist.text: Rats eat eggs, chicks, and seeds. On some islands they've even killed adult seabirds.

climax.text: Removing invasive predators is the single most effective action for island conservation.

punchline.text: Remove the predator and the island recovers.

quiz.question: What is the most effective island conservation action?
